result: I would dissolve the illusion that anyone is separate from anyone else—make the fact of our interdependence feel as obvious and urgent as thirst. Not a rule, not a slogan, just a lived, body-level certainty that your pain lowers the temperature in every room I’ll ever enter, that my joy literally alters the chemistry of your blood. If that single shift in felt reality were possible, the rest—war, greed, the slow suffocation of the planet—would collapse for the same reason you don’t keep hacking at your own knee once you notice it’s yours.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
